name: El Grado and Barasona Reservoirs Loop
name_original: De Embalse del Grado hasta Embalse de Barasona — circular por la Ribagorza
description: Cycle a difficult 23.8-mile circular route through Ribagorza, gaining 2401 feet with views of Olvena Canyon and the Grado I reservoir.
region_intro: Touring cycling around El Grado features routes that navigate through a landscape characterized by reservoirs, dams, and varied terrain. The region offers a mix of challenging climbs and more gentle paths, often connecting local towns and villages. This area in Huesca provides diverse options for cyclists to explore its natural and rural settings. Best touring cycling routes around El Grado The most popular touring cycling route is El Grado and Barasona Reservoirs Loop, a 23.8 miles (38.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 42 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the local reservoirs. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Puente de Abajo – Plaza Mayor de Graus loop from Barasona, an easy 8.0 miles (12.9 km) path. This route connects small towns and can be completed in under an hour. Local touring cyclists also love the Puente de Abajo – Plaza Mayor de Graus loop from La Puebla de Castro, a 19.0 miles (30.6 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and connecting villages, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Touring cycling around El Grado is defined by routes around reservoirs, through rural areas, and connecting historic towns. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more difficult climbs. The routes in El Grado are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore El Grado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around El Grado?
A: There are over 95 touring cycling routes available around El Grado, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around El Grado?
A: Touring cycling around El Grado features routes that navigate through a landscape characterized by reservoirs, dams, and varied terrain. You'll find a mix of challenging climbs and more gentle paths, often connecting local towns and villages. The area provides diverse options to explore its natural and rural settings.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in El Grado?
A: Yes, El Grado offers easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Abajo – Plaza Mayor de Graus loop from Barasona&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 8.0-mile (12.9 km) path that connects small towns and can be completed in under an hour.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in El Grado?
A: Absolutely. El Grado has 55 difficult touring cycling routes for experienced riders. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979434543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Grado and Barasona Reservoirs Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 23.8-mile (38.2 km) trail with significant elevation gain, offering views of the local reservoirs.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in El Grado?
A: The touring cycling routes in El Grado are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views of reservoirs, and the opportunity to connect between charming local towns and villages.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in El Grado?
A: Yes, many routes around El Grado are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Abajo – Plaza Mayor de Graus loop from La Puebla de Castro&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through rural landscapes and connects villages, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17546340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Naval loop from Naval&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes in El Grado?
A: Along the touring cycling routes, you can explore various natural attractions. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5286442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozán de Vero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/973469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picamartillo Cave&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2984940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Portal de Cunarga: Stone Arch&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer stunning views of the region's reservoirs.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area around El Grado offers several viewpoints. You can find scenic spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5284340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Torreciudad Sanctuary and the Grado I reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4990014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Barasona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3576844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mipanas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, providing excellent photo opportunities.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by historical or cultural landmarks?
A: While the primary focus is on natural landscapes and reservoirs, many routes connect local towns and villages, offering glimpses into the region's rural life and architecture. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Puebla de Castro – El Grado Dam loop from La Puebla de Castro&lt;/a&gt; connects villages and passes by the El Grado Dam, which is a significant local structure.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in El Grado?
A: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in El Grado, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summer can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winter might bring colder temperatures, though cycling is possible year-round depending on personal preference and route choice.

Q: Are there options for parking near the touring cycling routes?
A: While specific parking information for every trailhead isn't detailed, routes often start from or pass through towns and villages where parking facilities are typically available. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for more precise parking suggestions.

faqs.question: How challenging is this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered difficult and requires a very good fitness level. It features constant ups and downs with an elevation gain of approximately 730 meters over 38.2 kilometers, making for a demanding cycling experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of paved surfaces. However, be prepared for the characteristic 'constant ups and downs' of the Ribagorza region, which includes mountain passes and narrow roads, despite the smooth pavement.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll pass several notable spots, including the El Grado Dam, the impressive Olvena Canyon, and the charming settlement of La Puebla de Castro. You can also enjoy Views of the Torreciudad Sanctuary and the Grado I reservoir.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is estimated to take around 2 hours and 42 minutes to complete for an experienced touring cyclist, covering a distance of 38.2 kilometers.

faqs.question: Is there parking available at the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, the starting point of the route near Embalse del Grado is conveniently located next to a parking lot, making access easy for cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal details are not provided, the Ribagorza region offers diverse landscapes and tranquil settings. Consider spring and autumn for milder temperatures and pleasant cycling conditions, avoiding the peak heat of summer.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog-friendliness for this specific cycling route is not available. It's always best to check local regulations and consider the demanding nature of the route before bringing pets.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned for cycling this route. It is generally accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect to immerse yourself in the natural beauty and cultural heritage of Aragon. The route offers scenic countryside, charming villages, and awe-inspiring vistas, exploring rural life. The broader region features diverse landscapes from the Pyrenees to extensive plains.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths, including sections of the Aran Pirineos. Camino de Santiago, La Ribagorza. BTT. Ruta 2. Labitolosa, Sendero Mariano, and Secastilla-Olvena, among others.

faqs.question: How busy are the roads on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Ribagorza region is known for its low-density road traffic, suggesting a peaceful setting for cycling. This allows for a more tranquil experience away from heavy vehicle congestion.
